Elk River Fish Passage Executive Summary

Elk River Fish Passage Al Irvine, R.P.Bio. 2020-11-22 Executive Summary This is a sample book written in Markdown. You can use anything that Pandocs Markdown supports, e.g., a math equation \(a^2 + b^2 = c^2\).

1 Introduction Table 1.1: Fish Barrier Scoring (Fish Passage Technical Working Group, 2011). Risk Embedded Value Outlet Drop (cm) Value SWR Value Slope (%) Value Length (m) Value LOW >30cm or >20% of diameter and continuous 0 <15 0 <1.0 0 <1 0 <15 0 MOD <30cm or 20% of diameter but continuous 5 15-30 5 1.0-1.3 3 1-3 5 15-30 3 HIGH No embedment or discontinuous 10 >30 10 >1.3 6 >3 10 >30 6
2 Background Here is a review of existing methods. 2.1 Subheader This will be a little report for each site

3 Methods Table 3.1: Fish Barrier Scoring (Fish Passage Technical Working Group 2011). Risk Embedded Value Outlet Drop (cm) Value SWR Value Slope (%) Value Length (m) Value LOW >30cm or >20% of diameter and continuous 0 <15 0 <1.0 0 <1 0 <15 0 MOD <30cm or 20% of diameter but continuous 5 15-30 5 1.0-1.3 3 1-3 5 15-30 3 HIGH No embedment or discontinuous 10 >30 10 >1.3 6 >3 10 >30 6 Table 3.2: Fish Barrier Scoring Results (Fish Passage Technical Working Group 2011). Cumlative Score Result 0-14 passable 15-19 potential barrier >20 barrier You can write citations, too. For example, we are using the bookdown package (Xie 2020) in this sample book, which was built on top of R Markdown and knitr (Xie 2015).
4 Applications Some significant applications are demonstrated in this chapter. 4.1 Example one 4.2 Example two
5 Final Words We have finished a nice book.

References Xie, Yihui. 2015. Dynamic Documents with R and Knitr. 2nd ed. Boca Raton, Florida: Chapman; Hall/CRC. http://yihui.name/knitr/. . 2020. Bookdown: Authoring Books and Technical Documents with R Markdown. https://github.com/rstudio/bookdown.

Attribute 1 Value 1 Attribute 2 Value 2 Date 2020-09-20 Crossing Sub Type Round Culvert PSCIS ID NA Diameter 0.65 Modelled ID 4602533 Length 11.00 Crew KP, AI Embed Yes UTM Zone 11 Depth Embedded 0.100 Easting 661172 Resemble Channel Yes Northing 5524451 Backwatered No Stream Grave creek Percent Backwatered NA Road NA Fill Depth 1.20 Road Tenure NA Outlet Drop 0.00 Channel Width NA Outlet Pool Depth 0.00 Stream Slope NA Inlet Drop No Beaver Activity No Slope 0.5 Habitat Value Low Valley Fill Deep Fill Photos: 4602533 Comments: Stream dry at time of survey, channel not visible for about 40 downstream. Water and channel appear about 65m downstream of crossing. Upstream side of crossing inlet inlet not visible, covered by road fill or deep organic debris. Channel upstream non-existent and dry. This system (labelled as Grave creek on the map) must be a side or old channel.
